skill.name: Error Detective
skill.description: Search logs and codebases for error patterns, stack traces, and anomalies. Correlates errors across systems and identifies root causes.
skill.summary: AI-powered error analysis specialist for debugging distributed systems and production incidents.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill with no executable code, network access, or filesystem operations. Static analysis scanned 0 files and detected no security patterns. The skill provides guidance for log analysis and error investigation without any attack vectors.

content.user_title: Detect and diagnose errors across your systems
content.value_statement: Production errors are hard to trace across distributed systems. This skill analyzes logs, correlates errors, and identifies root causes to accelerate debugging.
actual_capabilities: Parse and extract errors from log files using regex patterns
Analyze stack traces across multiple programming languages
Correlate errors across distributed services and time windows
Generate monitoring queries for Elasticsearch and Splunk
Identify error rate anomalies and failure cascades
Provide root cause hypotheses with supporting evidence
limitations: Cannot directly access live log streams or monitoring systems
Requires log data to be provided as input for analysis
Cannot execute fixes or deploy code changes
Analysis accuracy depends on log completeness and quality

use_cases.title: Production Incident Investigation
use_cases.description: Analyze error logs from a production outage to identify the root cause and timeline of failures across microservices.

use_cases.title: Debugging Intermittent Failures
use_cases.description: Correlate sporadic errors across application logs to find patterns and triggering conditions that cause intermittent bugs.

use_cases.title: Post-Mortem Analysis
use_cases.description: Review historical error data after an incident to understand failure chains and recommend prevention strategies.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Error Pattern Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this log excerpt and extract all error messages with their timestamps. Group similar errors and identify the most frequent error type.

prompt_templates.title: Stack Trace Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Examine this stack trace and identify the root cause of the failure. Explain which code path triggered the error and suggest fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Cross-Service Error Correlation
prompt_templates.prompt: I have logs from three microservices during an outage. Correlate errors by timestamp and identify which service failed first and caused the cascade.

prompt_templates.title: Monitoring Query Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate Elasticsearch and Splunk queries to detect this specific error pattern in production. Include alerting thresholds for error rate spikes.

output_examples.input: Log file with repeated NullPointerException errors
output_examples.output: Extracted 47 NullPointerException occurrences between 14:32-14:45 UTC. Peak frequency: 12 errors/minute at 14:38. All errors originate from UserService.getUser() method. Correlation: errors began 2 minutes after deployment v2.3.1.

output_examples.input: Stack trace from payment service timeout
output_examples.output: Root cause: Database connection pool exhaustion. Evidence: Timeout at ConnectionPool.getConnection (line 142), preceded by 200+ pending requests. Fix: Increase pool size from 10 to 50 connections and add circuit breaker.

best_practices: Always include timestamps and correlation IDs when providing log samples for analysis
Share logs from all affected services to enable accurate cross-system correlation
Provide context about recent deployments or configuration changes that may relate to errors
anti_patterns: Do not share sensitive data like API keys, passwords, or personal information in logs
Avoid analyzing isolated error messages without surrounding log context
Do not assume the first visible error is the root cause - trace backward through the chain

faq.question: What log formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill can analyze any text-based log format including JSON logs, Apache/Nginx access logs, application logs, and structured logging outputs.

faq.question: Can this skill connect to my monitoring systems directly?
faq.answer: No, this is a prompt-based skill. You need to copy and paste log data or query results for analysis.

faq.question: How much log data should I provide for analysis?
faq.answer: Provide a representative sample around the error timeframe - typically 100-500 lines. For correlation, include logs from all affected services during the same time window.

faq.question: Can this skill help prevent future errors?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill provides prevention strategies based on identified root causes, including code changes, monitoring improvements, and architectural recommendations.

faq.question: Does this skill work with custom application logs?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill uses pattern recognition and regex analysis that works with any log format, including custom application-specific logging.

faq.question: What programming languages are supported for stack trace analysis?
faq.answer: The skill can analyze stack traces from Java, Python, JavaScript/Node.js, Ruby, Go, Rust, and other common languages with recognizable stack trace formats.
